video.kind: reduced offline animation candidate; not full 463-frame replica

camera_path.intent: Gate C push + orbit + close wipe rhythm retimed to 121 frames

dragon_path.source_honesty: Dragon remains the disclosed Gate C segmented proxy. It is motion/pass blocking only and must be replaced or polished before final visual acceptance.

frames.transition_role: stage and temple contact locked before dragon foreground pressure
dragon begins orbit behind the temple while the stage owns the silhouette
right arc reveal with rail, grass, and roof still owning hard edges
first foreground crossing, with temple/base contact preserved
dragon body becomes the near-lens wipe
largest mid-sequence occluder, hiding the push transition
dragon releases the wipe; integrated temple/base must remain readable
landmark push holds while dragon tail keeps irregular edge pressure
dragon head prepares final occluder; stage remains visible behind it
final dragon occluder preview; not accepted as final T06 art

limitations: not the full 463-frame Tiantan reference replica
dragon is a disclosed segmented proxy, not final T05 art
alpha is derived from source-rendered pass-id frames, not from flattened beauty
WeChat runtime is blocked until offline Gate D visual review accepts this candidate

visual_acceptance.reason: Independent visual review rejected Gate D v01. The candidate does not read as a vertical video window, and source honesty is acceptable, but the dragon proxy dominates key occlusion frames, f072/f116/f117 full coverage reads as a low-poly proxy wipe, and near-camera stage/railing/vegetation finish is not strong enough for full 463-frame production.
visual_acceptance.next_gate: Gate D v02 repair: rebuild or replace the dragon occluder, rework high/full coverage wipe windows, improve near-camera stage/railing/grass/temple finish, and submit a new reduced 121-frame package for independent offline review.
